title: The Great Gatsby
shortDescription: A story about the young and mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and his quixotic passion and obsession with the beautiful former debutante Daisy Buchanan.
A dystopian social science fiction novel and cautionary tale about totalitarianism, mass surveillance, and repressive regimentation.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
shortDescription: A novel about the roots of human behavior—to innocence and experience, kindness and cruelty, love and hatred, humor and pathos.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
shortDescription: A story of a few days in the life of a teenager Holden Caulfield in New York City.

title: Brave New World
shortDescription: A dystopian novel exploring a futuristic society where citizens are environmentally engineered into a social hierarchy.

title: The Hobbit
shortDescription: The adventure of Bilbo Baggins as he sets out on a quest to reclaim a lost kingdom.

title: Fahrenheit 451
shortDescription: A story about a future American society where books are outlawed and firemen burn any that are found.

title: Pride and Prejudice
shortDescription: A romantic novel of manners following Elizabeth Bennet as she deals with issues of manners, upbringing, morality, and education.

title: The Chronicles of Narnia
shortDescription: A series of fantasy novels set in the fictional realm of Narnia.

title: Lord of the Flies
shortDescription: A novel about a group of British boys stranded on an uninhabited island and their disastrous attempt to govern themselves.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
shortDescription: A multi-generational story of the Buendía family, whose patriarch, José Arcadio Buendía, founded the town of Macondo.
